item.title: Re: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: Noting that the new image used in this article is based off of this image, edited by AgentAX, and is cc-compliant.
It's guilty of the "Everything happens in the US" trope, but it's done so well that one can look over it. Upvoted and congrats on the +100!

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
The image on this article was created by myself using Pixlr, with the wreath asset taken from here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Nova_Roma_Flag.svg

However, as Wikipedia states:

This image only consists of simple geometric shapes or text. It does not meet the threshold of originality needed for copyright protection, and is therefore in the public domain. Although it is free of copyright restrictions, this image may still be subject to other restrictions.

The font used for the script beneath the "SPQR" initials is a variant of the Cherokee Unicode: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cherokee_(Unicode_block).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The threat of harsher penalties is given here, so my read is this was the initial punitive step that is legally justified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) As a punishment from Alexylva, this seems kind of underwhelming. Is this the most they can manage, or do they think it's enough punishment?

2) Wait, wire-fraud? Do they think we're sending misleading messages to get their stuff diverted to our universe?

item.title: Amazing
item.content:encoded: This SCP is amazing. You got my upvote. When I tried writing 2487, it sucked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really really really helpful. Thank you very very very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll provide some translations.

Sylvanos means "of forests"— Alexandria of Forests is where Alexylva University is located.

Aeternum means "forever"- so, the flag's epithet is "Alexandria of Forests Forever".

Tanasi Alternus = Alternate Tennessee. Tanasi is the Cherokee word from which we get the state of Tennessee.

Delphina Superbus is something that I made up—- the file for the sundial doesn't list a specific combine related to it, so I just made up something related to Delphi (where the Greek Oracles were) and Superb.

"Gimnasion" means "Gymnasium"— in some European languages, this is another word for secondary school. (I think.)

"Echus" just means Echo.

E: Few other things.

The initialism under "SPQR" is in Cherokee script, and stands for "Senate of (the) Cherokee and Roman Nation". If I got this wrong, someone please correct me and I will amend it as soon as possible.

Molisolus is a combination of "Molo" (to mill or grind) and "solus" (alone).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would someone kindly to translate these Latin or Greek-like words?
I NO NOTHING ABOUT LATIN! Or Greece.
sorry but because of this, I even can't understand what this article trying to tell.

Like, what the hell is this ALTERNUS? SYLVANOS？AETERNUM? Are they some kind of Latin words?

And, these GIMNASION, ECHUS, DELPHINIA SUPERBUS, are they just some Greek-like names, or is there any specific meaning for them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty much on the level of why Earth hasn't visited their galactic zoning office to protest the building of the new highway during the designated time it was posted for public review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never really cared about Alexylva, but this might be changing my mind.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely hoping that threat turns into something bigger. Alexylva doesn't get enough love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But that implies that they haven't considered that the postal service norms of other universes don't include multi-dimensional teleporters. Which is almost funnier, honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is great! Some of these containment procedures seem a little… overkill for just really strong wolves. The wolves are described as genetically modified. Are they just genetically deviated, or has it been confirmed that they have been altered? If there are humans coming from this thing, shouldn't there be an interview for at least one of them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, they might acknowledge their teleporter system might have flaws… But consider that the Foundation is KEEPING and OPENING the faulty delivered mail without being the intended recipient.

At least we could return it to sender, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fifty (50)

This is a good skip but all of these instances hurt my soul. ;-;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot to mention this in the critique forum, but I think my favorite part here is that clearly we must be stealing their junk because there's no way their teleporter system could possibly have design flaws.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This bit is unclear:

Following this, another employee of the United States Postal Service that has handled mail which was also handled by SCP-2487-03 will become the next SCP-2487-03 instance.

Does this mean another wolf appears, or that the next mailman will be simply be eaten without further manifestations? I'm thinking the latter but the way you defined -03 makes it ambiguous.

Regardless, I quite like this. Particularly how Alexylva seem to think this is going to cripple our mail service.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jane's is a publisher of hardback/physical annual guides on military equipment, things something like Jane's Aircraft Recognition Guide, with increasing specificity. We had them around as kids, don't know why.

I think they have an online presence, but Global Security is usually my go-to when Wikipedia doesn't cut it. It can be kind of a pain to navigate, but the articles aren't bad once you find them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, you wrote a University skip. :D I'm so happy!

On reflection, the weakness of this might be that it serves mostly to tie back to a bunch of previous Alexylva stuff, and explicitly. But my first reaction was "Letter carriers have enough problems as it is", so I don't mind leaving that excited upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, at least now we know who let the dogs out.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last part was a joke— I mainly just can't find reliable/easy-to-read sources. Also, what exactly is a "Jane's"?

(Yes, I am a pleb. Sue me.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's about damn time alexylva got a Series 3 skip (this is genuinely the first).
Take my upvote, and with it, you reach +40.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My father was career Postal Service, which makes this almost personal. The idea of the AU sending monster wolves into another universe to wreak revenge on mail thieves is the sort of thing that keeps me coming back to the Foundation.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What can I say, this is basically literary perfection.

If you really mean this, then you're in for quite the treat if you browse through the later pages of Top Rated Pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What can I say, this is basically literary perfection. Despite its length i was compelled to read the entire thing and as i saw details about 03 i immediately laughed - someone has finally made the sterotypical mailman getting chased by dogs into a scip!

Upvoted :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was almost going to upvote just because this basically takes place right in my backyard, but you did a fine job of mixing very disparate elements (portals, superdogs, postal employees, ALEXYLVA) and meshed them together almost effortlessly. +I!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could just say "anti-tank mines (fused for 60 kg targets)" or something. Or not worry about it much, I figured either the modifications made these things as heavy as a car or the mines were HEAT warheads but with anti-personnel fuses, or something.

Edit: Reading Wikipedia pages on military equipment or Global Security or even checking out a Jane's from your local library probably doesn't put you on a watch list. If it did that list would be uselessly long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am incapable of doing math, and also suck at finding sources on military equipment without fear of ending up on a watchlist. Will fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anti-tank mines

Are these wolves significantly denser than the 60-68 kg Canon dirus of prehistory? Anti-vehicular mine fuses generally require hundreds or thousands of kilograms to detonate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to apologize, I got the names of most of the combines mentioned in the final note wrong. They have been rectified. Also, I missed the fact that the Phitransimun Combine was the postal service, not a manufacturing corporation. I am sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So Alexylva finally got sick of the Foundation's shit, as they continued to pluck anomalies from their mail.

ATTEMPTING TO INTERRUPT THIS SENTENCE WILL RESULT IN HARSHER PUNISHMENT.

For some reason I'm imagining their authorities comes rushing in the Foundation-verse, only to be able to reach a peaceful compromise. Or we could have an article where they're mega-pissed, which could be cool as well.

item.title: #LOL
item.content:encoded: I used SCP-2487 for an SCP that failed and got deleted, but you made an even better one with more details, etc!!! XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is goofy-yet-terrifying, which is my favorite kind of terrifying. Also, I appreciate the presence of American languages in the letter; cultural absorption was one of the things that the Romans were really, really good at, and it's something that gets left out frequently, despite being one of the more interesting aspects of AU. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You managed to do a "dogs hate mailmen" SCP, and make it work. +1

item.title: Re: Vulgate Author (Supposed to mean Author Commentary, but Google Translate is dumb)
item.content:encoded:
I'm no latin scholar, except i am, a terrible one, and my terrible latin sense is telling me that that says 'common author' or something along those lines. Something better would be along the lines of "scriptor scriptura" although that isn't declined.

Great job on this, btw.

item.title: Vulgate Author (Supposed to mean Author Commentary, but Google Translate is dumb)
item.content:encoded:
Well then, this is a thing. Following the recent debate regarding the GOI list (which I will not go into here), I noticed that quite a few GOIs on the list have been neglected for quite some time, ALEXYLVA being one of them. We've never really seen anything from this world without the University's perspective being in play, and while they are still present, they're mostly angry about mail fraud.

Next stop: the UIU! (Maybe. Possibly.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congrats, you ignited my interest in a goi that i previously had absolutely no liking for whatsoever

Have an upboat

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely bizarre, and I really really like it!
